commit.message:
Initial scaffold: Katana SaaS — extension + Next.js web app

- extension/: Chrome MV3 extension adapted from katana-prototype
  - Auth-token gated (Supabase) instead of API key
  - service-worker.js POSTs to /api/grade instead of calling Claude directly
  - sidepanel: sign-in/out flow, account status card
- web/: Next.js 15 app with App Router
  - Landing page with pricing (Free / Pro / Department)
  - Magic-link auth via Supabase; /auth/callback sends token to extension
  - /api/grade: validates auth, checks quota, calls Claude, increments counter
  - Dashboard: usage, plan, Chrome Web Store link
  - Stripe client + price ID constants
  - CORS headers for chrome-extension:// origin

commit.message:
fix: read org_id from URL param in project creation handler

PostRequest previously required organization_id as a body field, but
the BFF only sends name/slug/description — org_id is already in the
URL as {org_id}. Removed the redundant body field and updated the
handler to use requtil.ParseUUID(r, "org_id") instead. Tests updated
to inject org_id as a chi URL param and cover both missing and invalid
param cases.

Fix Codex provider: enable file writes with workspace-write sandbox mode

The Codex SDK defaults to read-only sandbox mode, which prevented it from
writing or editing files. Added sandboxMode: 'workspace-write' and
approvalPolicy: 'never' to the thread options.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: inject dev env vars in make run-api and run-web targets

make run-api and run-all were missing DEV_BYPASS_RBAC, so RBAC
middleware rejected every BFF request with 401 when running locally
outside Docker (compose.override.yaml only applies inside containers).

- run-api / run-all: pass DEV_BYPASS_RBAC=true to go run so all RBAC
  role checks are bypassed in the local dev environment
- run-web / run-all: pass API_AUTH_TOKEN=dev-token to go run so the
  BFF sends a valid Bearer header without manual env setup

No code changes — only Makefile recipe env var placement.

commit.message:
fix: populate WHO log fields eagerly and emit 4xx/5xx at warn/error level

Two improvements to the 5W1H HTTP log schema:

1. WHO fields on failed requests were empty (user_id="", org_id="")
   because RequireRole only wrote RequestLog after all checks passed.
   Fix: write rl.OrgID as soon as org_id is parsed from the URL, and
   rl.UserID as soon as the X-User-ID header is parsed — before any
   error return. Even 401/403 responses now carry the identity context
   that was available at the time of rejection.
   DEV_BYPASS_RBAC path also captures org_id from URL when present.

2. All http.request log entries were emitted at INFO level regardless
   of the HTTP status code. Fix: emit at Warn (4xx) or Error (5xx) so
   alerting pipelines that filter by log level (CloudWatch Logs,
   Datadog, Loki) catch client/server errors without extra query logic.
   2xx/3xx remain INFO (normal traffic).

Tests added:
- TestLogLevelForStatus: verifies Info/Warn/Error selection per status range
- TestLogger_RequestLogPropagation: table-driven, covers success (all WHO
  fields set), partial 401 (org_id known, user_id empty), and no-auth path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rename Fathom toggle to Yaniv's Notetaker in popup UI

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: populate RequestLog WHO fields eagerly in RequireRole

Move RequestLogFrom to the top of RequireRole so org_id and user_id
are written to the per-request log as soon as each value is resolved,
rather than only on the success path. This means failed requests
(bad org_id, unauthorized user, membership not found) now carry
maximum identity context in the http.request log entry, making
incident debugging easier.

Also captures org_id from URL during DEV_BYPASS_RBAC so bypass
requests appear with org context in logs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: update rules and fix DEV_BYPASS_RBAC zero-UUID bug

Distill recurring issues from post-commit analysis reports into
actionable coding rules and fix the one live bug found in the process.

**Docs (.claude/rules/ + CLAUDE.md)**
- architecture.md: add Pagination requirement, SSE header-before-error
  pattern, DEV_BYPASS_RBAC contract, HTMX swap alignment, safe hx-vals,
  CSS/CDN SRI guidance, url.Values requirement
- go-patterns.md: add integer path param → BadRequestError mapping,
  json.Marshal error handling, url.Values for query strings, typed
  APIClient request/response structs, slug regex consistency, SDK paths
- testing.md: add specific status code assertions, loop-variable mutation
  anti-pattern, t.Setenv parallelism rule, template helper testing table,
  partial handler test requirement, build artifact hygiene
- tdd.md: add DO/DON'T items matching the above, Coverage Gaps table,
  PR checklist additions
- CLAUDE.md: Known Pitfalls table, DEV_BYPASS_RBAC dev setup notes,
  HTMX swap rules, template helper testing note, SDK clarifications

**Bug fix (rbac.go + cmd/main.go)**
- DEV_BYPASS_RBAC bypass now injects both RoleOwner AND devBypassUserID
  into context; previously only role was set, causing GetUserID(ctx) to
  return uuid.UUID{},false for all bypassed requests — silently producing
  zero UUIDs in any handler that calls GetUserID for logging or filtering
- Added startup Warn log when DEV_BYPASS_RBAC=true so the bypass is
  visible in container logs and impossible to overlook

**Test (rbac_test.go)**
- TestDevBypassInjectsSyntheticUserID: verifies GetUserID returns the
  well-known dev UUID with ok=true when bypass is active

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: enable pointer-events on share card buttons after animation

GSAP's fromTo sets opacity:0 inline initially; without explicit
pointer-events management the buttons could remain unresponsive if
the tween was interrupted or staggered. Now sets pointer-events:none
during animation and restores auto on complete.

Also fixes dl-btn canvas still rendering 'SAMIK' → 'BOCHYA'.

Fix Codex "not inside trusted directory" error

Pass skipGitRepoCheck and workingDirectory to startThread()
instead of using the hacky process.chdir() workaround.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: redesign logging with unified 5W1H structured schema

Replace ad-hoc logger calls with a consistent 5W1H (Who-What-When-
Where-Why-How) schema across every log emission in the API.

## Design

Every log entry maps fields to five dimensions:

  who:   user_id, org_id, auth (bearer|apikey|bypass), ip
  what:  action (e.g. "GET /organizations/{org_slug}")
  when:  duration_ms
  where: layer (http|middleware|handler|startup), component
  why:   outcome (success|client_error|server_error), status/error
  how:   method, path, route, request_id, user_agent, query

## Key changes

- `apps/pkgs/logger`: Add RequestLog mutable pointer stored in context.
  Each middleware layer writes WHO fields (user_id, org_id, auth_method)
  onto the same pointer; the HTTP Logger reads them after the request
  completes — zero mutex needed, one pointer per request.

- `middleware/logger.go`: Pre-creates chi RouteContext so chi fills in
  the route pattern in-place, giving cardinality-safe action labels
  (e.g. "/organizations/{org_slug}" not "/organizations/acme-corp").
  Emits a single "http.request" entry with all 5W1H groups as nested
  JSON. Adds outcomeFromStatus and stripAPIPrefix helpers.

- `middleware/bearer_auth.go`: Sets rl.AuthMethod = "bearer" on success;
  structured "auth.token_validation_failed" error log with where/why/how.

- `middleware/apikey.go`: Sets rl.AuthMethod = "apikey" and rl.OrgID on
  success; structured error logs for lookup failure (ERROR) and
  last_used update failure (WARN, best-effort).

- `middleware/rbac.go`: Sets rl.UserID, rl.OrgID, rl.AuthMethod="bypass"
  on success/bypass; structured "rbac.membership_lookup_failed" with
  who/where/why/how.

- `cmd/main.go`: All lifecycle events use dot-notation event names
  (server.start, server.shutdown, db.connect_failed, cache.enabled, etc.)
  with where/why slog.Group fields.

## Tests added

- logger_test.go: TestOutcomeFromStatus, TestStripAPIPrefix,
  TestLogger_RequestLogPropagation
- apps/pkgs/logger/logger_test.go: TestNewRequestLog, TestRequestLogFrom,
  TestRequestLog_SharedPointer (proves pointer-in-context mutation pattern)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Major accuracy upgrade: 2026 AI detection knowledge + consensus scoring

- Switch back to claude-sonnet-4-6 (haiku was losing too many subtle signals)
- Completely rewrite image prompt: Flux/MJ v7/DALL-E 3 specific artifacts,
  biological signals, lens forensics, composition analysis
- Completely rewrite video prompt: Sora/Kling/Runway/HunyuanVideo/Pika signatures,
  biological motion (breathing, micro-tremors, eye movement), physics analysis
- Temporal diff: higher resolution (480x854), compute mean/std statistics per diff
  and pass as numeric context to Claude (mean <3 = suspicious near-zero = AI signal)
- Consensus scoring: if 2+ detectors agree (>=50%), add +6-12 confidence boost
- Video prompt now references 2026 generator-specific tells by name

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix session persistence and enhance UI design

Session fix:
- Auth callback now sets cookies directly on redirect response object
  instead of relying on cookies() API which doesn't carry over to redirects
- Middleware now copies auth cookies to all redirect responses via
  redirectWithCookies helper, preventing session loss on navigation

Design enhancements (Remotion-inspired):
- Sidebar nav items with 3D border/shadow effects on hover and active state
- Dashboard cards with elevation shadows and hover transitions
- Upload card as primary CTA with arrow animation
- Search bar with bordered input style
- Bolder typography throughout (font-black, tighter tracking)
- Header with backdrop blur effect
- User footer with bordered card style

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: set recap_email_sent=True even when 0 emails sent

Prevents infinite retry loop when SMTP is misconfigured. The flag now
marks the attempt as complete regardless of delivery count, with a
warning log when 0 emails were sent so admins can investigate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

only, table-based layout, system font stacks, no <style> blocks.

  plain text and HTML parts for graceful fallback

  pick result, season rank, and top-3 weekly leaderboard table

- _app_context() helper avoids nested session issues when called
  from sync_api.py CLI commands vs standalone scheduled tasks

  in all finalization paths (sync-earnings, sync-run results/earnings)

commit.message:
Fix snap-to-ground accumulation during engine-controlled movement

- Suppress snap-to-ground when prevRequestedVelocity.y >= 0 (strict check)
  to avoid snapping during movePlayerTo interpolation where requested
  velocity is zero
- Reset prevRequestedVelocity alongside velocity when an external override
  is detected, so snap is also suppressed on that same tick
- Remove debug movePlayerTo test system

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add null guard to SurvivorAnims AnimSetInt to prevent GetHealth01 crash

When Expansion AI proxy entities flood the engine with GetHealth01 errors
on the client, engine state can become corrupted. The next AnimSetInt call
on the local player's ModCommandHandlerAfter then crashes with an access
violation at a corrupted heap address. Adding a null check on
GetAnimInterface() prevents the crash.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: enable BFF→API communication in dev via DEV_BYPASS_RBAC

The web BFF was getting 401s from RBAC-protected routes because
RequireRole requires X-User-ID + DB membership, which the BFF
cannot provide without JWT/Cognito integration.

- Add DEV_BYPASS_RBAC=true bypass in RequireRole (injects RoleOwner,
  skips all identity/membership checks when env var is set)
- Set DEV_BYPASS_RBAC=true in compose.override.yaml and .example
- Read API_AUTH_TOKEN from env in web NewAPIClient (falls back to
  "dev-token" when unset)
- Add TestRequireRole/DEV_BYPASS_RBAC test case using t.Setenv
- Add TestNewAPIClient_AuthHeader tests for the token env var logic

fix: security hardening, scrollbar fix, and bump version to 0.3.2

- Prevent prototype pollution via CSV headers (__proto__, constructor, prototype)
- Restrict SQL queries to SELECT-only, blocking DROP/DELETE/INSERT/UPDATE/etc.
- Add webview message origin validation and edit payload type/size checks
- Replace Math.random() nonce with crypto.randomBytes()
- Add localResourceRoots to restrict webview resource access
- Replace innerHTML with safe DOM methods in autocomplete
- Fix file descriptor leak in readRange() with try/finally
- Fix scrollbar not draggable due to error ruler intercepting pointer events
- Remove unused diagnostics collection
- Add *.vsix to .vscodeignore
- Resolve all npm audit vulnerabilities
- Add security regression test suite (81 tests)

Pressing the user button while a profile is active now causes the robot
to stop immediately rather than continuing to run indefinitely.

- motion.h: add emergency_stop() which calls reset_drive_system() then
  disable_drive(). This is the shared safe-state entry point for the
  motion layer.
- motion.h: spin_turn() forward-bleed loop and wait_until_position()
  both check switches.button_pressed() and call emergency_stop() on press.
- profile.h: wait_until_finished() checks switches.button_pressed() and
  calls this->stop() (target speed → 0, state → PS_FINISHED), causing
  the systick PID to ramp motors down. Returns immediately so the caller
  can handle the abort.
- mouse.h: panic() now calls motion.emergency_stop() instead of the two
  separate reset_drive_system()/disable_drive() calls. Sensor shutdown
  and operator interaction (LED blink, button wait) remain in panic().

Closes #25 (FR-10)
